name: Randomized ambroxol PDD trial showed target engagement but did not confirm cognitive benefit
summary: A randomized human trial strengthens target-engagement evidence while limiting functional-benefit claims.
statement: In a 55-participant randomized Parkinson disease dementia trial, ambroxol was safe and tolerated and demonstrated beta-glucocerebrosidase target engagement, but there was no evidence of group differences on primary or secondary cognitive and clinical outcomes.
population_or_model: Patients older than 50 years with Parkinson disease dementia
quantitative_note: The high-dose group had higher beta-glucocerebrosidase levels at week 26 than placebo, while gastrointestinal adverse events were more frequent with ambroxol.
caveats: The trial was modest in size and single-center.
The target-engagement result is disease-specific and does not establish broad aging benefit.
Null primary outcomes prevent upgrading the track to human functional benefit.
